---
title: "Variance refactor: single IF engine for all methods"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
---

## Context

causatr has scattered variance code: `gcomp_variance_sandwich()` uses the
full IF, `ice_compute_influence()` reimplements the same pattern for
longitudinal data, and IPW/matching use a $J V_\beta J^T$ shortcut that breaks
for non-static interventions (Phase 4). The refactor replaces everything with
one engine.

## Theory

Every causal estimand $\hat\mu$ is defined by a system of estimating equations
(model scores + mean equation). The IF is the sandwich applied to this system,
with the block-triangular bread inverted by back-substitution:

$$
\text{IF}_i = \underbrace{(\text{pred}_i - \hat\mu)}_
{\text{Channel 1 (1 line of code)}}
\;+\;
\underbrace{\sum_k \color{#31A354}{J_k}
\;\color{#3182BD}{A_k^{-1}}
\;\psi_{k,i}}_{\text{Channel 2 (one term per model } k\text{)}}
$$

$$
\widehat{\text{Var}}(\hat\mu) = \frac{1}{n^2}\sum_i \text{IF}_i^2
$$

Channel 2 calls `correct_model()` once per model $k$. See
`vignettes/variance-theory.qmd` Sections 3–4 for the full derivation.

## The process

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Architecture of variance_if(). Channel 1 is universal. Channel 2 dispatches by method. Aggregation is standard or cluster-robust."
flowchart TB
  CH1["<b>Channel 1</b> (universal, 1 line)<br/>IF_i = (n/n_t) × (pred_i − μ̂)"]

  GC["<b>g-comp</b><br/>1 model<br/>correct_model()"]
  IPW["<b>IPW</b><br/>2 models<br/>correct_propensity()<br/>(wraps correct_model)"]
  MATCH["<b>matching</b><br/>1 model<br/>correct_model()"]
  ICE["<b>ICE</b><br/>K+1 models<br/>correct_model() in a loop<br/>(sensitivities propagate)"]

  CH1 --> SUM["IF = Ch.1 + Σ Ch.2 corrections"]
  GC --> SUM
  IPW --> SUM
  MATCH --> SUM
  ICE --> SUM

  SUM --> AGG["<b>Aggregate</b><br/>vcov_from_if(IF_list, cluster)"]
  AGG --> OUT["k × k variance–covariance matrix"]

  style CH1 fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#31A354
  style GC fill:#f0f4ff,stroke:#3182BD
  style IPW fill:#f0f4ff,stroke:#3182BD
  style MATCH fill:#f0f4ff,stroke:#3182BD
  style ICE fill:#f0f4ff,stroke:#3182BD
  style SUM fill:#fff5eb,stroke:#E6550D
  style AGG fill:#fff5eb,stroke:#E6550D
  style OUT fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#31A354
```

### What `correct_model()` does

Every Channel 2 term follows the same 3-line pattern:

$$
\underset{p \times 1}{h} = \color{#3182BD}{A^{-1}} \cdot
\color{#31A354}{g}
\qquad
\underset{n \times 1}{d} = X h
\qquad
\text{correction}_i = n \cdot d_i \cdot r^{\text{score}}_i
$$

where $\color{#31A354}{g}$ is the gradient (Jacobian),
$\color{#3182BD}{A^{-1}} = (X^TWX)^{-1}$ is the bread inverse,
$d_i$ is individual $i$'s sensitivity, and $r^{\text{score}}_i$ is
their **score residual** (see next paragraph). This is
$\color{#31A354}{J_k}\color{#3182BD}{A_k^{-1}}\psi_{k,i}$ projected to
a scalar per individual.

**The score residual, not the response residual.** The GLM score is
$\psi_i = (Y_i - \mu_i) \cdot (d\mu/d\eta)/V(\mu) \cdot X_i$
(vignette Section 1.2, route 3). For **canonical** links the factor
$(d\mu/d\eta)/V(\mu) = 1$ and $\psi_i$ simplifies to response residual
times $X_i$. For **non-canonical** links (probit, cloglog, Gamma-log,
Poisson-sqrt, etc.) it does not simplify, and `correct_model()` must
multiply the response residual by $(d\mu/d\eta)/V(\mu)$ before using
it. This is a one-line fix over the existing `model_if_correction()`,
which silently assumes canonical links.

```r
correct_model <- function(model, gradient, fit_idx, n_total) {
  X_fit <- model.matrix(model)
  B_inv <- bread_inv(model, X_fit)   # (X'WX)^{-1}

  h <- as.numeric(B_inv %*% gradient)
  d <- as.numeric(X_fit %*% h)

  # Score residual = response residual × (dmu/deta) / V(mu).
  # For canonical links this factor is 1; for non-canonical it is not,
  # and using the raw response residual gives a miscalibrated IF.
  eta    <- model$linear.predictors
  mu_eta <- model$family$mu.eta(eta)
  var_mu <- model$family$variance(stats::fitted(model))
  r      <- stats::residuals(model, type = "response") * mu_eta / var_mu

  out <- rep(0, n_total)
  out[fit_idx] <- n_total * d * r
  list(correction = out, d = d, h = h)  # ICE needs d; IPW needs h
}
```

Point-treatment methods use `$correction`. ICE also uses `$d` to
compute the next model's gradient. IPW Branch B uses `$h` to build the
propensity correction's derived gradient (see `correct_propensity()`
section).

For **GAMs** (`mgcv::gam`), `model$linear.predictors`,
`model$family$mu.eta`, and `model$family$variance` all exist and the
same code path works unchanged. For models where these slots don't
exist, `variance_if_numeric()` takes over.

## Functions (7 total)

Seven functions live inside `R/variance_if.R` after the refactor.

### `variance_if()`

- **Status:** new. Replaces `variance_sandwich()`,
  `gcomp_variance_sandwich()`, and `ice_variance_sandwich()`.
- **Purpose:** entry point. Channel 1 inline, Channel 2 dispatch,
  aggregate.
- **Called by:** `compute_contrast()`.
- **Calls:** `iv_design_matrix()`, `correct_model()`,
  `correct_propensity()`, `variance_if_numeric()`, `vcov_from_if()`.

### `correct_model()`

- **Status:** renamed from `model_if_correction`; now returns
  sensitivities `d` and bread-projected gradient `h` alongside the
  correction vector.
- **Purpose:** core Channel 2 primitive. Given a gradient $g$,
  computes $h = A^{-1} g$, $d_i = X_i h$, and
  $\text{correction}_i = n \cdot d_i \cdot r_i$.
- **Called by:**
    - `variance_if()` on the g-comp, matching, and ICE branches
      (once each for g-comp and matching; $K{+}1$ times in a loop
      for ICE).
    - `correct_propensity()` Branch B — twice, once for the MSM
      correction term and once for the propensity correction term
      with a derived gradient.
- **Calls:** `bread_inv()`.

### `correct_propensity()`

- **Status:** new.
- **Purpose:** IPW-specific Channel 2 helper. Dispatches internally
  into two branches depending on whether the fit uses WeightIt or a
  self-contained weighted GLM.
- **Called by:** `variance_if()` on the IPW branch only.
- **Calls:**
    - **Branch A (WeightIt, Phase 3):** `sandwich::estfun(asympt = TRUE)`,
      `sandwich::bread()`.
    - **Branch B (self-contained, Phase 4):** `correct_model()` twice
      (MSM term + propensity term via derived gradient),
      `numDeriv::jacobian()` on the averaged weighted score.

### `bread_inv()`

- **Status:** renamed from `model_bread_inv`.
- **Purpose:** $(X^T W X)^{-1}$ for GLMs (via IWLS working weights);
  `model$Vp` for GAMs.
- **Called by:**
    - `correct_model()` on every call.
    - `correct_propensity()` Branch B for the propensity model's bread.
- **Calls:** model introspection only — `$family$mu.eta`,
  `$family$variance`, or `$Vp` depending on the model class.

### `iv_design_matrix()`

- **Status:** renamed from `intervention_design_matrix`.
- **Purpose:** builds $X^*$ for counterfactual data. Handles the GLM /
  GAM split.
- **Called by:**
    - `variance_if()` once per intervention, before dispatching to a
      method branch.
    - `variance_if_numeric()` on the non-GLM fallback path.
- **Calls:** `stats::model.matrix()` for GLMs;
  `stats::predict(type = "lpmatrix")` for GAMs.

### `vcov_from_if()`

- **Status:** extended with an optional `cluster` argument.
- **Purpose:** aggregates per-individual influence functions into a
  $k \times k$ variance–covariance matrix:
  $(1/n^2) \sum_i \text{IF}_{a,i} \cdot \text{IF}_{b,i}$. With
  `cluster = subclass`, sums IFs within each matched subclass before
  squaring (cluster-robust aggregation for matching).
- **Called by:** `variance_if()` once at the end of the main path.
  **Not** called by `variance_if_numeric()`, which returns a vcov
  directly.
- **Calls:** pure R matrix operations only — no further dispatch.

### `variance_if_numeric()`

- **Status:** renamed from `compute_vcov_marginal`, rewritten as a
  two-tier fallback.
- **Purpose:** non-GLM fallback. Two tiers:
    - **Tier 1 — `sandwich::estfun` path.** If
      `sandwich::estfun(fit$model)` exists, it returns the
      per-observation score matrix $\psi_i$. Combine with
      `sandwich::bread(fit$model) / nobs(fit$model)` to get
      per-observation $\text{IF}_\beta = A^{-1}\psi_i$, project
      through the marginal-mean Jacobian $J$ (computed via
      `numDeriv::jacobian` on `predict()`), add to Channel 1,
      aggregate via the normal `vcov_from_if()` path. **Recovers the
      full IF exactly** — same cross-term handling as the main path.
    - **Tier 2 — plain delta shortcut.** If `estfun` does not exist
      (truly exotic model classes), compute Channel 1 variance
      directly as $(1/n^2)\sum_i t_i^2 (\text{pred}_i - \hat\mu)^2$
      and Channel 2 as $J V_\beta J^T$, and return their sum. Emit a
      `rlang::warn()` that the cross-term
      $(2/n^2)\sum_i \text{Ch.1}_i \cdot \text{Ch.2}_i$ has been
      dropped and the variance may be slightly miscalibrated (vignette
      Section 3.3).
- **Called by:** `variance_if()` when `bread_inv()` is not analytic
  (no `$family$mu.eta` and no `$Vp`).
- **Calls:**
    - **Tier 1:** `iv_design_matrix()`, `numDeriv::jacobian()` on
      `predict()`, `sandwich::estfun()`, `sandwich::bread()`,
      `vcov_from_if()`.
    - **Tier 2:** `iv_design_matrix()`, `numDeriv::jacobian()`, the
      model's `vcov()`.

Functions **deleted**: `variance_sandwich()`, `gcomp_variance_sandwich()`,
`ice_variance_sandwich()`, `ice_compute_influence()`.

`compute_vcov_marginal()` is **not** deleted — it is renamed
`variance_if_numeric()` and kept as the fallback branch of `variance_if()`
for model classes that do not expose analytic bread / mu.eta.

No standalone `compute_channel1()` or `compute_gradient()` — these are
one-liners that live inline inside `variance_if()`.

## Where numerical differentiation earns its keep

Most of the refactor uses analytic derivatives — they are simpler *and*
faster than numerical ones for GLMs/GAMs, because the link derivative
`family$mu.eta` and the bread $(X^TWX)^{-1}$ are available in closed form.
Numerical differentiation (`numDeriv::grad`, `numDeriv::jacobian`) is used
in exactly **two** places, both of which are genuine simplifications:

1. **`correct_propensity()` self-contained path (Phase 4).** The weight
   function $w_i(\alpha) = g^*(A_i\mid L_i; \alpha)/g(A_i\mid L_i; \alpha)$
   changes per intervention type (static, MTP-shift, MTP-scale, IPSI,
   dynamic rule). Deriving the cross-derivative $A_{\beta\alpha}$
   symbolically for each combination is tedious and error-prone. A
   single `numDeriv::jacobian` call on the averaged weighted score
   (as a function of $\alpha$, with $\beta$ fixed) returns the whole
   $p \times q$ matrix and generalises cleanly across all current and
   future intervention types.

2. **`variance_if_numeric()` fallback (Tier 1 and Tier 2).** For model
   classes that do not expose `family$mu.eta` / `family$variance` or
   `$Vp` (custom `model_fn`, non-standard GAM families, `geeglm`,
   `betareg`, `polr`, etc.), `numDeriv::jacobian` computes $J$ from
   `predict(model, newdata)`. From there the function tries two paths:

    - **Tier 1 (preferred)**: if `sandwich::estfun(model)` exists, it
      provides $\psi_i$ per observation. Combined with
      `sandwich::bread(model)`, we get $\text{IF}_{\beta,i}$ per
      observation, project through $J$, add to Channel 1, aggregate via
      `vcov_from_if()`. **This matches the main-path IF exactly** — no
      cross-term drop — whenever `sandwich::estfun` has a method for
      the model class.
    - **Tier 2 (warn and undercount)**: if no `estfun` method, fall
      back to computing $V_1 + V_2 = (1/n^2)\sum_i \text{Ch.1}_i^2 +
      J V_\beta J^T$ and return their sum. This drops the cross-term
      $(2/n^2)\sum_i \text{Ch.1}_i \text{Ch.2}_i$ from the vignette's
      Section 3.3 decomposition. Emit a warning so the user knows the
      variance is slightly miscalibrated for exotic models.

   Why not use `sandwich::estfun` for **everything** (the main path
   too)? Because the main path needs $d_i = X_i \cdot h$ for ICE's
   recursive sensitivity propagation, which requires `model.matrix()`
   — not `estfun()`, which hides $X_i$ behind the score product. We
   would still need `model.matrix()` alongside `estfun()`, so no
   simplification is gained. The analytic `correct_model()` path is
   also faster in the hot bootstrap loop and has a more permissive
   contract (any family with `mu.eta` / `variance`, which is broader
   than "has an `estfun` method").

Everywhere else — gradients, bread inverses, ICE recursive sensitivities —
the analytic form is kept. Tests in the next sections pin down the
equivalence between analytic and numerical paths at specific known
quantities so regressions are caught immediately.

## How each method works inside `variance_if()`

### G-comp (1 model)

$$
\text{IF}_i = \underbrace{g^{-1}(X_i^{*T}\hat\beta) - \hat\mu_a}_
{\text{Channel 1}}
+ \underbrace{\color{#31A354}{J_a}
\color{#3182BD}{A_\beta^{-1}}
\psi_{\beta,i}}_{\text{1 model correction}}
$$

```r
IF <- (n / n_t) * target * (pred - mu_hat)
gradient <- crossprod(X_star, mu_eta_star * w) / sum_w
IF <- IF + correct_model(fit$model, gradient, fit_idx, n)$correction
```

### IPW (2 models, two branches)

$$
\text{IF}_i = \underbrace{g^{-1}(X_i^{*T}\hat\beta) - \hat\mu_a}_
{\text{Channel 1}}
+ \underbrace{\color{#31A354}{J}
\color{#E6550D}{A_{\beta\beta}^{-1}}
\psi_{\beta,i}}_{\text{MSM correction}}
+ \underbrace{\color{#31A354}{J}
\color{#E6550D}{A_{\beta\beta}^{-1}}
\color{#E6550D}{A_{\beta\alpha}}
\color{#3182BD}{A_{\alpha\alpha}^{-1}}
\psi_{\alpha,i}}_{\text{propensity correction}}
$$

`correct_propensity()` dispatches internally based on the fit object
(see its dedicated section below). The caller code in `variance_if()`
is agnostic:

```r
IF <- (n / n_t) * target * (pred - mu_hat)
J  <- crossprod(X_star, mu_eta_star * w) / sum_w        # marginal-mean Jacobian
IF <- IF + correct_propensity(fit, J, fit_idx, n)
```

`correct_propensity()` returns the **combined** Channel 2 contribution
(MSM correction + propensity correction) for IPW. There is no separate
`correct_model(msm_model, ...)` call in the IPW branch — everything is
folded into `correct_propensity()`:

- **Branch A (WeightIt).** `sandwich::estfun(asympt = TRUE)` returns the
  adjusted score matrix, which already contains both the MSM score and
  the propensity-uncertainty correction (Wooldridge 2010). Multiplying
  by `bread(msm)` and then by $J$ yields Ch.2_MSM + Ch.2_propensity in
  one pass.
- **Branch B (self-contained).** `correct_propensity()` calls
  `correct_model(msm_model, J, ...)` internally for the MSM correction,
  then computes the propensity correction via `numDeriv::jacobian` on
  the weighted score (see its dedicated section).

### Matching (1 model, cluster-robust)

$$
\text{IF}_i = \underbrace{g^{-1}(X_i^{*T}\hat\beta) - \hat\mu_a}_
{\text{Channel 1}}
+ \underbrace{\color{#31A354}{J_a}
\color{#3182BD}{A_\beta^{-1}}
\psi_{\beta,i}}_{\text{1 model correction}}
\qquad\qquad
\widehat{\text{Var}} = \frac{1}{n^2}\sum_{c=1}^C
\left(\sum_{i \in c} \text{IF}_i\right)^2
$$

```r
IF <- (n / n_t) * target * (pred - mu_hat)
gradient <- crossprod(X_star, mu_eta_star * w) / sum_w
IF <- IF + correct_model(fit$model, gradient, fit_idx, n)$correction
vcov_from_if(IF_list, n, names, cluster = subclass)
```

### ICE ($K\!+\!1$ models, backward recursion)

$$
\text{IF}_i = \underbrace{\frac{1}{n_t}(\tilde Y_{0,i} - \hat\mu)}_
{\text{Channel 1}}
+ \sum_{k=0}^K
\underbrace{n_k \cdot \color{#E6550D}{d_{k,i}} \cdot r_{k,i}}_
{\text{model } k \text{ correction via } \texttt{correct\_model()}}
$$

```r
IF <- (n / n_t) * target * (pseudo_final - mu_hat)
d_vec <- rep(0, n)
for (step in seq_len(K + 1)) {
  gradient <- if (step == 1) {
    crossprod(X_star, mu_eta_star * w) / sum_w       # base case
  } else {
    crossprod(X_star, d_prev * mu_eta_star)           # recursive
  }
  result <- correct_model(models[[step]], gradient, fit_ids[[step]], n)
  IF <- IF + result$correction
  d_vec <- result$d
}
```

## Changes to `compute_contrast()` (`R/contrast.R`)

Replace two separate call sites (`R/contrast.R:533–537` for the ICE path
and `R/contrast.R:598–603` for the point-treatment path) with one:

```r
if (ci_method == "sandwich") {
  vcov_mat <- variance_if(fit, data_a_list, preds_list, mu_hat,
                          target_idx, ice_results = ice_results)
}
```

The longitudinal path still runs `ice_iterate()` first. The point-treatment
path still runs `apply_intervention()` + `predict()`. But the variance call
is one line in both cases.

## `correct_propensity()` — the IPW-specific piece

Captures how $\hat\mu$ depends on the propensity parameters $\alpha$
through the weights:

$$
\text{IF}_i \mathrel{+}=
\color{#31A354}{J}
\;\color{#E6550D}{A_{\beta\beta}^{-1}}
\;\color{#E6550D}{A_{\beta\alpha}}
\;\color{#3182BD}{A_{\alpha\alpha}^{-1}}
\;\psi_{\alpha,i}
$$

WeightIt does not support the non-static interventions (dynamic, MTP,
IPSI) that Phase 4 will introduce. `correct_propensity()` therefore has
**two branches**, dispatched by whether `fit$model` is a `glm_weightit`
or a self-contained weighted GLM:

### Branch A: WeightIt shortcut (Phase 3, static interventions)

`WeightIt::glm_weightit()` already implements the stacked M-estimation
correction internally (Wooldridge 2010 eq. 12.41). We do not need to
touch WeightIt internals or compute $A_{\beta\alpha}$ ourselves —
`sandwich::estfun(asympt = TRUE)` returns the adjusted score matrix and
`sandwich::bread()` returns the bread:

```r
E <- sandwich::estfun(fit$model, asympt = TRUE)       # n_fit × p, adjusted
B <- sandwich::bread(fit$model) / stats::nobs(fit$model)  # p × p
IF_beta <- E %*% B                                    # per-obs IF(β̂)
IF_mu[fit_idx] <- IF_mu[fit_idx] + as.numeric(IF_beta %*% J)
```

Five lines, covers binary / multinomial / continuous static IPW with any
Mparts-supporting method (`glm`, `cbps`, `ipt`, `ebal`). See
`WeightIt/R/glm_weightit-methods.R:621–790`.

**Guardrail.** Non-Mparts methods (`gbm`, `super`, `bart`, `optweight`,
`energy`, `npcbps`) silently set `asympt = FALSE` inside WeightIt, which
treats weights as fixed. Detect at **fit time** via
`is.null(attr(weightit_obj, "Mparts"))` and either (a) `rlang::warn()`
that SEs ignore weight-estimation uncertainty, or (b) refuse
`ci_method = "sandwich"` and require bootstrap. Do not defer this to
contrast time — users should learn about the limitation early.

### Branch B: Self-contained (Phase 4, dynamic / MTP / IPSI)

`fit$model` is a plain weighted GLM; `fit$details$propensity_model` holds
the propensity GLM we fitted ourselves; `fit$details$weight_fn` is an R
closure $\alpha \mapsto w_i(\alpha)$ that reconstructs weights given the
propensity parameters.

Steps:

1. **MSM correction term.** Call `correct_model(msm_model, J, fit_idx, n)`
   exactly as for g-comp. This covers the middle term of the three-term
   IPW IF — it is *not* specific to the propensity correction.
2. **Cross-derivative $\color{#E6550D}{A_{\beta\alpha}}$ via
   `numDeriv::jacobian`.** Build a function
   $\alpha \mapsto (1/n)\sum_i \psi_{\beta,i}(\alpha,\hat\beta)$ (the
   weighted MSM score as a function of the propensity parameters, with
   $\beta$ held at $\hat\beta$) and differentiate numerically:

    ```r
    psi_beta_bar <- function(alpha) {
      w <- weight_fn(alpha)                 # length n, reconstructed weights
      eta <- X %*% beta_hat
      mu  <- family$linkinv(eta)
      mu_eta <- family$mu.eta(eta)
      r   <- Y - mu
      as.numeric(crossprod(X, w * mu_eta * r / V(mu))) / n
    }
    # A_{βα} = -(1/n) Σ ∂ψ_β,i/∂α^T. numDeriv gives +∂(average)/∂α,
    # so flip the sign:
    A_beta_alpha <- -numDeriv::jacobian(psi_beta_bar, x = alpha_hat)  # p × q
    ```

3. **Propensity correction term via a derived gradient.** The propensity
   contribution to $\text{IF}_i$ is
   $\color{#31A354}{J}
   \color{#E6550D}{A_{\beta\beta}^{-1}}
   \color{#E6550D}{A_{\beta\alpha}}
   \color{#3182BD}{A_{\alpha\alpha}^{-1}}
   \psi_{\alpha,i}$.
   Let $h_\text{msm} = \color{#E6550D}{A_{\beta\beta}^{-1}} \color{#31A354}{J^T}$
   (a $p$-vector — already computed by `correct_model()` on the MSM in
   step 1). Define the derived gradient
   $g^\text{prop} = \color{#E6550D}{A_{\beta\alpha}^T} h_\text{msm}$
   (a $q$-vector), then call
   `correct_model(propensity_model, g_prop, fit_idx, n)` — this reuses
   the same 5-line helper and produces the per-observation propensity
   correction without any new primitives. The result is added to `IF`.

**Implementation note.** Step 1's `correct_model()` already returned
`h = A_{ββ}^{-1} × gradient`, so extract it directly from that call
rather than recomputing. A small extension to `correct_model()` —
returning `h` alongside `$correction` and `$d` — keeps everything
symmetric and cheap.

**Why `numDeriv::jacobian` here and not an analytic formula?** Because
the weight function $w_i(\alpha)$ is intervention-specific: binary static
has one form, shift MTPs another, IPSI a third, dynamic rules a fourth.
Each analytic derivative is ~10 lines, but wrong for the other three.
A single `numDeriv::jacobian` call replaces all of them and is exact to
Richardson-extrapolation tolerance (default `~1e-8`). For the common
binary static case we verify the numerical derivative against the
closed-form derivative as a test (see next section).

### Tests: analytic vs numerical equivalence

Three tests pin down `correct_propensity()` against known-correct
quantities. All three should pass at machine precision (or at numDeriv's
Richardson tolerance, `~1e-7`).

**Test 1 — WeightIt shortcut vs. self-contained numerical (static binary,
logistic propensity).** The same fit can be expressed both ways:

- Branch A: fit via `WeightIt::weightit(A ~ L, method = "glm")` → feed to
  the `estfun`/`bread` shortcut.
- Branch B: fit the logistic propensity manually with `stats::glm`,
  compute weights by hand, fit a weighted MSM with `stats::glm`, feed to
  the self-contained `correct_propensity()` using `numDeriv::jacobian`.

Both paths must yield the same `IF_mu`, SE, and vcov to
`~1e-7` absolute tolerance (the numerical derivative's accuracy floor).

**Test 2 — Numerical $A_{\beta\alpha}$ vs. closed-form (binary static,
logistic propensity).** For logistic propensity $\pi_i(\alpha) =
\text{expit}(L_i^T\alpha)$ and binary static weights
$w_i = A_i/\pi_i + (1-A_i)/(1-\pi_i)$, the closed-form derivative is:

$$
\frac{\partial w_i}{\partial \alpha}
= -\frac{A_i (1-\pi_i)}{\pi_i}\,L_i
+ \frac{(1-A_i)\,\pi_i}{1-\pi_i}\,L_i
$$

Compose into $\partial\psi_{\beta,i}/\partial\alpha^T$ (weighted GLM
score with $\beta$ fixed) and average to form the closed-form
$A_{\beta\alpha}$. Compare against `numDeriv::jacobian(psi_beta_bar, ...)`
— elementwise equality to `~1e-7`. This is a one-off sanity test, not a
runtime path: it lives in `tests/testthat/test-variance-if.R` and guards
the numerical approach for the simplest case we can fully derive by hand.

**Test 3 — Final vcov vs. hand-computed stacked sandwich (linear Gaussian
case).** For a linear outcome model fit by weighted least squares with
logistic propensity, the full $(q + p + 1) \times (q + p + 1)$ stacked
bread and meat have closed forms and can be assembled by hand on a tiny
simulated dataset ($n = 200$, $q = p = 2$). Invert, read off the bottom
row, and compare the resulting $\text{Var}(\hat\mu_a)$ to
`variance_if()`'s output at `~1e-10`. This is the end-to-end sanity check
that the whole `variance_if()` pipeline agrees with a brute-force
M-estimation reference — slow to compute, but unambiguous.

## Documentation changes

### `vignettes/variance-theory.qmd`

Already amended (see Sections 3.5, 3.6, 3.7, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 5.4, 5.5,
5.6, 6). Key additions and fixes:

- **Section 3.5**: stacked EE $\to$ block-triangular bread $\to$
  back-substitution = IF.
- **Section 3.6** *(new)*: target subpopulation derivation. Justifies
  the $(n/n_t)$ scaling factor on Channel 1 for ATT / ATU / custom
  subsets from the $A_{\mu\mu} = n_t/n$ block of the stacked bread.
  Derives @eq-if-gcomp-target as a strict generalisation of
  @eq-if-gcomp.
- **Section 3.7** (pseudocode): updated to use the **score residual**
  $r \cdot \mu_\eta / V$ instead of the plain response residual, so
  non-canonical links produce the correct IF. Also adds a comment
  explaining the $(n/n_t)$ and $n$ factors inline.
- **Section 4.2**: full three-term IPW IF derivation.
- **Section 4.3**: cluster-robust IF aggregation for matching.
- **Section 4.4**: unified summary table (all methods use IF).
- **Section 5.4** (base case, recursive case): updated to use the
  score residual $r^{\text{score}}_{k,i}$ instead of $r_{k,i}$ in the
  ICE correction formula. Added a callout warning that explains why
  the plain response residual is wrong for non-canonical links.
- **Section 5.5** (scaling): updated formula from
  $n_k \cdot d_{k,i} \cdot r_{k,i}$ to
  $n_k \cdot d_{k,i} \cdot r^{\text{score}}_{k,i}$.
- **Section 5.6** (pseudocode): `chained_influence()` updated to
  compute `r_score_k = r_response_k * mu_eta_k / V_k` before
  accumulating the IF, with an inline comment explaining when the
  factor is nontrivial.

### `CLAUDE.md`

Update architecture notes:

- Single `variance_if()` entry point
- `correct_model()` is the shared building block
- `correct_propensity()` for IPW — two branches (WeightIt shortcut +
  self-contained numerical)
- `vcov_from_if(cluster = ...)` for matching
- `variance_if_numeric()` as the non-GLM fallback (renamed from
  `compute_vcov_marginal`)
- Remove references to method-specific dispatch
  (`variance_sandwich` / `gcomp_variance_sandwich` /
  `ice_variance_sandwich`)

### Method vignettes

Light touch: update `vignettes/gcomp.qmd`, `ipw.qmd`, `matching.qmd` to say
"influence function" instead of "sandwich + delta method".

### Roxygen

`variance_if()` roxygen `@description` references vignette Sections 3–4.
`correct_model()` roxygen explains bread $\times$ gradient $\times$ score.

## Step-by-step

1. Rename `model_bread_inv` $\to$ `bread_inv`,
   `intervention_design_matrix` $\to$ `iv_design_matrix`
2. Refactor `model_if_correction` $\to$ `correct_model`. Three changes:
   (a) return `d` and `h` alongside `correction`; (b) replace the
   response residual with the **score residual** $r \cdot \mu_\eta/V$
   so non-canonical links are handled correctly (C2); (c) write the
   regression test that compares against `sandwich::estfun` on a probit
   GLM before moving on.
3. Rename `compute_vcov_marginal` $\to$ `variance_if_numeric` and
   rewrite as a **two-tier fallback**: Tier 1 tries
   `sandwich::estfun(model)` + `bread(model)` to recover the full IF
   through the normal `vcov_from_if()` path; Tier 2 falls back to
   $V_1 + V_2$ with a warning about the dropped cross-term (C3).
4. Write `variance_if()` with g-comp path (migrate from
   `gcomp_variance_sandwich`) and wire the `variance_if_numeric()`
   dispatch for non-GLM models
5. Add matching path to `variance_if()` (extend `vcov_from_if` with cluster)
6. Add ICE path to `variance_if()` (rewrite `ice_compute_influence` using
   `correct_model`)
7. Write `correct_propensity()` Branch A (WeightIt shortcut via
   `sandwich::estfun(asympt = TRUE)`) and add IPW path to `variance_if()`
8. Add the fit-time Mparts guardrail in `causat(method = "ipw")`
9. Write `correct_propensity()` Branch B (self-contained, with
   `numDeriv::jacobian` for $A_{\beta\alpha}$) — **deferred to Phase 4
   kickoff** but scaffold the dispatcher now so Phase 3 is unblocked
10. Update `compute_contrast()` call sites
11. Delete superseded functions (see "Functions" table — note
    `compute_vcov_marginal` is renamed, not deleted)
12. Add the analytic-vs-numerical tests (see `correct_propensity()`
    tests subsection) and the other tests listed in D7
13. Run `devtools::test()` — existing + new tests must pass
14. Update CLAUDE.md, vignette, roxygen
15. Run `devtools::document()` and `devtools::test()` again

## Call graph

Two small diagrams. The first shows where `variance_if()` sits in the
public API. The second shows what the refactor builds inside it.
Only `causat()` and `contrast()` are user-callable (green); everything
else is internal. Boxes marked **★** are new or rewritten.

### Where `variance_if()` sits

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "variance_if() is invoked from inside compute_contrast() when ci_method = 'sandwich'. The bootstrap path is unchanged."
flowchart LR
  U((user)) -->|"step 1"| C["causat()"]:::user
  C --> F[("causatr_fit")]
  F --> CC
  U -->|"step 2"| T["contrast()"]:::user
  T --> CC["compute_contrast()"]:::int
  CC -.->|"ci = sandwich"| VIF["variance_if() ★"]:::new
  CC -.->|"ci = bootstrap"| VB["variance_bootstrap()"]:::int
  VIF --> R[("causatr_result")]
  VB --> R
  R --> U

  classDef user fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#1b5e20,stroke-width:3px
  classDef int fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0
  classDef new fill:#ffe0b2,stroke:#e64a19,stroke-width:2px
```

### Inside `variance_if()`

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "correct_model() is the shared primitive, called by g-comp/matching/ICE and by correct_propensity() Branch B. vcov_from_if() is called by variance_if() at the end of the main path; variance_if_numeric() is a parallel fallback that returns its own vcov. iv_design_matrix() and bread_inv() are low-level utilities (see Functions table)."
flowchart TD
  VIF["variance_if() ★"]:::new
  VIF -.->|"g-comp / matching / ICE"| CM["correct_model() ★"]:::new
  VIF -.->|"IPW"| CP["correct_propensity() ★"]:::new
  VIF -.->|"non-GLM fallback"| VIFN["variance_if_numeric() ★"]:::new
  VIF --> VFIF["vcov_from_if() ★"]:::new

  CP -.->|"Branch A<br/>(WeightIt)"| SAND["sandwich::estfun<br/>sandwich::bread"]:::ext
  CP -.->|"Branch B<br/>(self-contained)"| CM
  CP -.->|"Branch B: A_βα"| NJ["numDeriv::jacobian"]:::ext
  VIFN --> NJ

  CM --> BI["bread_inv() ★"]:::new

  classDef new fill:#ffe0b2,stroke:#e64a19,stroke-width:2px
  classDef ext fill:#fff9c4,stroke:#f57f17
```

### Key points

1. **`correct_model()` is the load-bearing primitive** — called by four
   paths: g-comp (once), matching (once), ICE (K+1 times in a loop),
   and IPW Branch B (twice). Test it in isolation first.
2. **`correct_propensity()` is a dispatcher, not a computation.**
   Branch A uses `sandwich::estfun(asympt = TRUE)`; Branch B uses
   `numDeriv::jacobian` on the weighted score plus two `correct_model()`
   calls. They must agree for static + logistic propensity (Test 1).
3. **`numDeriv::jacobian` appears in exactly two places**:
   `correct_propensity()` Branch B and `variance_if_numeric()`.
   Anywhere else during implementation means something is over-abstracted.
4. **Bootstrap is independent.** `variance_bootstrap()` refits the
   whole pipeline on resampled data and never touches `variance_if()`.
   The refactor leaves it alone.

## What does NOT change

- Bootstrap code (`variance_bootstrap.R`)
- Fitting code (`R/gcomp.R`, `R/ipw.R`, `R/matching.R`, `R/ice.R`)
- User-facing API (`contrast()`, `causat()`)

## Doubts / implementation-time checklist

Flagged during design review (2026-04-13). Re-check each of these at the
corresponding step — they are things that are *easy to get wrong* and
that the main plan glosses over by necessity.

### D1. Matching: cluster-robust aggregation needs a validation test

`vcov_from_if(IF_list, n, names, cluster = subclass)` is new code.
Before trusting it:

1. Fit a matched saturated MSM ($Y \sim A$) with a static intervention
   so Channel 1 = 0 by construction.
2. Compare `vcov_from_if(..., cluster = subclass)` against
   `sandwich::vcovCL(matched_model, cluster = ~subclass)` propagated via
   $J V_\beta J^T$.

They must agree to machine precision. Any discrepancy signals a bug in
the clustering indexing, the `n / n_t` Channel 1 scaling (which must use
the **matched** sample size, not the original), or the `fit_idx`
alignment against the matched frame.

### D2. ICE sensitivity ordering is counterintuitive — document it

Models are fit **backward** in time (K → 0). Sensitivities are computed
**forward** (0 → K) inside `variance_if()`. Both orderings are correct —
they correspond to the two directions of inverting a block-triangular
bread matrix — but a reader of the loop will assume the directions
match. Add a short roxygen / inline comment pointing at vignette
Section 5.4.

### D3. Aggregation is not uniform across methods

The "IF = Ch.1 + Σ Ch.2" diagram is true **per individual**. But
matching aggregates differently (cluster-robust: sum IFs within a matched
subclass before squaring). Make sure the diagram and roxygen do not
imply that `vcov_from_if()` takes the same code path in all four cases —
the `cluster` argument is load-bearing.

### D4. `correct_model()` returns `$d` always — is the overhead material?

For g-comp and matching, `$d` is computed but never read. The allocation
is $O(n)$ per call. Benchmark on the NHEFS dataset ($n = 1629$) — if it
is <1% of total variance-computation time, leave it for symmetry.
Otherwise add a `return_d = FALSE` default and flip it on only inside
the ICE loop.

### D5. `numDeriv` tolerance is not machine precision

The analytic-vs-numerical tests for `correct_propensity()` must use an
absolute tolerance around `1e-7`, not `.Machine$double.eps`.
`numDeriv::jacobian` uses Richardson extrapolation over a finite step
and caps out around `1e-8` on well-conditioned problems. The tests
should assert `< 1e-6` to leave headroom for conditioning issues in CI.

### D6. Mparts guardrail must fire at fit time, not contrast time

The non-Mparts WeightIt methods (`gbm`, `super`, `bart`, `optweight`,
`energy`, `npcbps`) silently drop the propensity-uncertainty correction.
If we defer the warning to `contrast(ci_method = "sandwich")`, users who
run `diagnose()` or `summary()` first will not see it. Emit the warning
inside `causat(method = "ipw")` immediately after the `weightit()` call
completes.

### D7. Required new tests before merging

Existing tests passing is necessary but not sufficient. At minimum:

- **Non-canonical link correctness (C2 fix).** Fit a probit binomial
  GLM and a Gamma-log GLM on simulated data; compare `correct_model()`
  output against `sandwich::estfun(model) %*% bread(model) / nobs(model)`
  projected through $J$. They must agree to machine precision.
  Repeat the comparison against the **old** canonical-only formula
  ($n \cdot d \cdot r_{\text{response}}$, without the
  $\mu_\eta/V$ scaling) and assert that the old formula **disagrees**
  materially — this guards against silent regression if someone
  removes the scale factor "to match the vignette".
- **WeightIt shortcut vs. self-contained numerical** `correct_propensity()`
  on a static binary IPW with logistic propensity — agree to `~1e-7`.
- **Analytic vs. numerical $A_{\beta\alpha}$** on the same setup — agree
  to `~1e-7`. (Test 2 in the `correct_propensity()` section.)
- **Matching IF path vs. `sandwich::vcovCL`** in the Channel 1 = 0
  setup — machine precision.
- **IPW with a non-Mparts WeightIt method** — expect a warning, not
  silent under-coverage.
- **ICE with $K = 1$ collapses to point-treatment g-comp** —
  `variance_if()` via the ICE branch and the g-comp branch must agree.
- **Non-static IPW intervention (Phase 4 pilot)** where Channel 1 ≠ 0 —
  IF-based variance must exceed $J V_\beta J^T$ by a meaningful amount
  on NHEFS.
- **`variance_if_numeric()` Tier 1 path.** Use a `glm` wrapped to hide
  `family$mu.eta` but keep `sandwich::estfun.glm` reachable. The Tier 1
  path via `estfun` + `bread` must match the analytic main path to
  machine precision.
- **`variance_if_numeric()` Tier 2 path.** Use a model class with
  neither `family$mu.eta` nor an `estfun` method (mock it with a stub).
  Expect a warning about the dropped cross-term, and check that the
  returned vcov equals $V_1 + V_2$ (Channel 1 variance + delta method)
  by hand on a tiny dataset.
